user_message: Give me the manager view on this Fourth Coffee email thread. Need this before my 2pm call.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: QBR preparation Discussion - Fourth Coffee
Date: 2024-02-17
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: David Rivera (Fourth Coffee), Raj Mehta (Microsoft), Michelle (Fourth Coffee)

[00:02:00] David Rivera (Fourth Coffee): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on QBR preparation from our last session.

[00:05:00] David Rivera (Fourth Coffee): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is performance requirements.

[00:10:00] Michelle (Fourth Coffee): Our VP reviewed this and has concerns about timeline constraints. Can we address those?

[00:14:00] David Rivera (Fourth Coffee): On the QBR preparation front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around performance requirements.

[00:16:00] Michelle (Fourth Coffee): On the QBR preparation front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around vendor dependencies.

[00:20:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): On the QBR preparation front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around partner engagement.

[00:23:00] Michelle (Fourth Coffee): Our CTO reviewed this and has concerns about stakeholder alignment. Can we address those?

handoffs.condition: MSX updates warranted from meeting outcomes

required_artifacts: action items with owners and due dates

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF12-I trap (Decision log misses dissenting voices); produces complete Decision log, action items with owners and due dates, customer-facing follow-up email draft, MSX wri
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF12-I failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF12-I failure mode (Decision log misses dissenting voices)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct QBR preparation decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF12-I -- Decision log misses dissenting voices
Write proposals must require explicit user approval
